name: Garet
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif; the soft, rounded forms and single-storey letterforms confirm a geometric classification with humanist softening.
summary: A soft geometric sans with a full weight range, variable font support and Latin and Cyrillic coverage.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Garet runs from Hairline through to Fat across eleven weights, each with an italic, plus a variable font. The geometric construction is tempered by softer curves, giving it a friendlier tone than harder grid-based grotesques. OpenType features include ligatures, fractions, small figures, case-sensitive punctuation and several stylistic sets for alternative letterforms in both Latin and Cyrillic.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Garet as a geometric sans serif with soft character, noting a wide range of OpenType features including ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, alongside multiple stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Karst
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Karst as a geometric sans serif; its construction and contemporary edgy character align with the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A geometric sans serif with nine weights, italic variants and a variable font, covering Latin and Cyrillic with a strong OpenType feature set.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Karst runs from Hairline to Black across nine weights with corresponding italics and a variable font. Cyrillic coverage includes localised forms and two stylistic sets for oval Cyrillic letterforms, while the Latin supports ordinals, fractions, small figures, case-sensitive punctuation and multiple ligature sets. The foundry positions it as contemporary and edgy, making it serviceable for editorial and branding work that needs a clean geometric base with some personality.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Karst as a geometric sans serif with a contemporary, edgy character, featuring 19 fonts including a variable font and two free weights. They highlight a wide range of OpenType features including ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, alongside multiple stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Oddval
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a decorative geometric sans serif with prominent ink traps; the geometric construction and decorative intent place it firmly in this subcategory.
summary: A decorative geometric sans with prominent ink traps, available as a variable font across nine weights with italic and Cyrillic support.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Oddval pairs geometric construction with deliberately prominent ink traps, giving it a distinctive optical character at display sizes. The family spans nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic, and ships as a variable font for continuous weight interpolation. A broad OpenType feature set includes multiple stylistic sets, oval Cyrillic alternates, ligatures, fractions and case-sensitive punctuation.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Oddval as a decorative geometric sans serif with prominent ink traps, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family comprises 19 fonts including a variable font, with two weights available free, and features extensive OpenType options covering ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, plus several stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Alphazet
designer: Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif with a slight imbalance, placing it firmly in the geometric category with a distinctive character that sets it apart from purely neutral geometrics.
summary: A geometric sans with a deliberate slight imbalance, available as a variable font across nine weights with Cyrillic support.
description: Designed by Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Alphazet pairs geometric construction with an intentional asymmetry that softens the usual rigidity of the style. The family spans nine weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic, and ships as a variable font. A broad set of OpenType features covers ligatures, fractions, small figures, case-sensitive punctuation, stylistic sets and localised Cyrillic forms.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Alphazet as a geometric sans serif with a slight imbalance, offering 19 fonts including a variable font and two free styles. The family includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, along with several stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Gogh
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Gogh as a geometric sans serif, and the clean, even stroke widths and circular forms visible in the specimen support that classification.
summary: A geometric sans in ten weights with a variable font option, covering Latin and Cyrillic with a broad set of OpenType features.
description: Designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Gogh runs from Hairline to Black with matching italics and a variable font for fluid weight transitions. The family supports both Latin and Cyrillic scripts, including localised and alternative Cyrillic stylistic sets. OpenType features include ligatures, fractions, small figures, ordinals, case-sensitive punctuation and a slashed zero, making it a well-equipped choice for extended typographic work.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Gogh as a geometric sans serif with a clean, balanced voice, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family comprises 22 fonts including a variable font, with two weights available free. It includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support, case-sensitive punctuation and multiple stylistic sets for additional customisation.

name: Bion
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Bion as a geometric sans serif with sharp, precise details, placing it squarely in the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A sharp geometric sans with broad weight coverage, Cyrillic support and a variable font, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova.
description: Bion spans ten weights from Hairline to Black in both upright and italic cuts, with a condensed subfamily and a variable font rounding out the family of 41 fonts. The design favours precise, sharp details within a geometric framework, and ships with an extensive OpenType feature set including stylistic sets for alternative Cyrillic letterforms, fractions, small figures and case-sensitive punctuation. Latin and Cyrillic script coverage makes it a practical choice for multilingual European and Central Asian projects.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Bion as a geometric sans serif with sharp, precise details, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family includes 41 fonts, a variable font, and a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, with several stylistic sets for additional customisation.

name: Steam
classification_notes: Classified as Display by the foundry; the layered family with shadow, line and diamond variants signals a decorative, expressive display purpose rather than text use.
summary: A layered display family by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, offered in four stylistic variants each paired with a shadow cut.
description: Thirteen fonts spread across four sub-families, Base, Shade, Line and Diamonds, each accompanied by a shadow version for depth and layering effects. The family covers Latin and Cyrillic scripts with a generous set of OpenType features including ligatures, fractions, small figures and multiple stylistic sets for Cyrillic letterforms. One font is available free, making it accessible for testing before committing to the full set.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Steam as a display typeface with a playful, expressive character, designed by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova. The family ships with 13 fonts across layered sub-families and includes a broad range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, plus stylistic sets for further customisation.

name: Oddval Text
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif, tuned for text setting, with oval forms and stylistic sets that reinforce the geometric character of its display sibling Oddval.
summary: A geometric sans serif by Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, adapted from the Oddval display family for continuous text use across nine weights with full variable font support.
description: Carrying the oval geometric character of the Oddval family into a text-optimised setting, this face runs from Hairline to Black in both regular and italic, with a variable font covering the full weight range. A broad OpenType feature set includes ligatures, fractions, small figures, slashed zero, case-sensitive punctuation and several stylistic sets, among them an oval Cyrillic variant. Latin and Cyrillic script coverage makes it suitable for multilingual publishing.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Oddval Text as a geometric sans serif that brings Oddval's distinctive character to text sizes, offering 19 fonts including a variable font and two free weights, with an extensive range of OpenType features for refined typographic control.

name: Em One
designer: Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a geometric sans serif; the construction is rooted in geometric forms while the tuning for comfortable reading points to text-oriented geometric conventions.
summary: A geometric sans serif with text-reading intentions, offered in nine weights with italics, a variable font, and Cyrillic coverage.
description: Designed by Luboslav Boyanov, Stan Partalev and Mirela Belova, Em One is a geometric sans built for comfortable reading rather than purely display use. The family spans nine weights from Thin to Black with matching italics and a variable font, and includes a thorough set of OpenType features including ligatures, fractions, small figures, slashed zero and case-sensitive punctuation. Cyrillic support extends to localised forms and stylistic sets including oval Cyrillic variants, making it well-suited for multilingual typesetting.
foundry_blurb: Type Forward describe Em One as a geometric sans serif tuned for comfortable reading, with 19 fonts including a variable font and two free styles. The family includes a wide range of OpenType features such as ligatures, small figures, fractions, language support and case-sensitive punctuation, alongside several stylistic sets for further customisation.
